mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and reduced household consumption capacity, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns exceed +1.5% in >70% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where INDPRO growth accelerates, or the signal shows no predictive power during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above the 215.0 threshold trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the inflation signal shows zero incremental predictive value over a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are negative in >55% of aligned PAYEMS/UNRATE observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 expands, or the correlation between GDP growth and equity returns falls below 0.10 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production contracts for two months, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in walk-forward tests.
